title: [cm/access-check] access

given: an actor and a resource with a server-side access rule
when: the resource is read/written under the eventual, selective-disclosure model
then: the value visible to the actor matches the source's access decision

why_non_obvious: intent-CM graded this access-check site 'hard': [access-check] The update is gated on an immediate server-side `access('update', $account, TRUE)` decision, so an eventually consistent or selectively disclosed permission view could authorize a revoked user or reject a newly authorized one unless access evaluation is coordinated or resolved.

title: [cm/unique-constraint] entityBundleFieldInfo

given: two records that each satisfy a server-side uniqueness invariant in isolation
when: both are created independently (offline / concurrent replicas)
then: the port's convergence rule for the uniqueness violation is observed and asserted

why_non_obvious: intent-CM graded this unique-constraint site 'hard': [unique-constraint] The `UniqueBirthLog` field constraint assumes globally unique birth-log values, which concurrent offline writes can violate under eventual consistency unless a convergence rule or coordination strategy resolves duplicates.

when: both updates are applied without a central lock

title: [source] A seeding or transplanting log is created as `pending` unless its individual `do

given: A planting submission includes a seeding, transplanting, or harvest log section.
The section's `done` value is absent or false.
when: The form is submitted.
then: The created log has status `pending`.

why_non_obvious: A quick form that records an event can easily be ported as immediately completed, and the checkbox label does not make the pending-by-default lifecycle policy obvious.

source_read.rule: A seeding or transplanting log is created as `pending` unless its individual `done` checkbox is truthy; creating the plant does not imply that its recorded events are completed.

title: [source] Seeding and transplanting logs are movement events, while harvest logs are expli

given: A planting submission creates one seeding log, one transplanting log, and one harvest log.
when: The form is submitted.
then: The seeding and transplanting logs have `is_movement = true`, while the harvest log has `is_movement = false`.

why_non_obvious: All three are generated as logs attached to the same plant asset, and both seeding and harvest may look like inventory-affecting events; only two types receive movement semantics.

source_read.rule: Seeding and transplanting logs are movement events, while harvest logs are explicitly non-movement events.

title: [source] A custom plant name takes effect only when the custom-name flag is truthy and a

given: A submitted form contains a `name` value.
The `custom_name` flag is false or absent.
when: The form is submitted.
then: The plant asset uses the generated name rather than the submitted `name` value.

why_non_obvious: Persisting a nonempty name field alone, or treating the generated name as merely an initial suggestion, changes the observable naming behavior.

source_read.rule: A custom plant name takes effect only when the custom-name flag is truthy and a `name` value exists; otherwise the name is regenerated from the form values.

title: [source] The generated plant name uses transplanting location in preference to seeding lo

given: The selected seasons are `2026` and `Summer`.
The crops are `Tomato` and `Basil`.
The seeding location is `Greenhouse`.
The transplanting location is `Field A`.
No custom name is supplied.
when: The plant asset name is generated.
then: The location component is `Field A`, not `Greenhouse`; season names are combined as `2026/Summer` and crop names as `Tomato, Basil`.

why_non_obvious: A port may choose the first entered location, merge both locations, or assume seeding is the canonical planting location. The code overwrites the earlier seeding-derived location during ordered traversal.

source_read.rule: The generated plant name uses transplanting location in preference to seeding location whenever both sections have location values; seasons are slash-joined and crops are comma-joined.

title: [source] An empty quantity value produces no quantity payload at all (`NULL`), including

given: A log section has quantity value `0` or an empty value.
Other log fields are valid.
when: The form is submitted.
then: `prepareQuantity` returns `NULL` for the quantity and the created log is passed `quantity: [NULL]`, rather than a zero-valued standard quantity.

why_non_obvious: A reimplementation may store zero as a measured quantity, or omit the entire `quantity` field rather than retaining the wrapper list containing the prepared result.

source_read.rule: An empty quantity value produces no quantity payload at all (`NULL`), including a textual/numeric zero value under PHP empty-value semantics; when a quantity exists, the log still receives a one-element quantity list.

title: [source] Previously selected seasons are remembered as defaults only after a created plan

given: The saved planting-season state contains season A.
A newly created plant resolves no season references.
when: The planting form is submitted.
then: The saved state is not updated to an empty list, so season A remains the future default.

why_non_obvious: “Remember selected seasons” might be implemented as latest-wins state, including clearing the stored preference when the new submission has none. This implementation writes state conditionally.

source_read.rule: Previously selected seasons are remembered as defaults only after a created plant has at least one resolved season reference; an empty selection does not overwrite/clear the saved season state.

title: [source] A harvest log may carry a single lot number as a string-valued field; it is opti

given: A harvest log exists.
The harvest is associated with an externally assigned lot identifier such as `LOT-2026-A`.
when: The lot identifier is recorded on the harvest.
then: The stored value preserves the identifier as a string rather than requiring numeric parsing or a batch-entity reference.

why_non_obvious: “Lot number” could be implemented as an integer or as a required relationship to a batch entity, but the source defines it specifically as a string field and supplies only descriptive guidance, not a required constraint.

source_read.rule: A harvest log may carry a single lot number as a string-valued field; it is optional metadata identifying a batch/lot rather than a numeric quantity.
